meta.notes: v2.9.4: Vol clamp (Fix 1b) implemented in execution gate. effective_vol = clamp(ewma_vol, 0.75×RV1y, 1.50×RV1y). RV1y = 252-day rolling std of daily log returns, annualised. Bounds validated against 2019–2026 history including 2020 COVID and 2022 rate shock onsets. Bank of Canada calm-window concern not empirically confirmed. All existing gate parameters (sigma thresholds, defer windows, per-ticker overrides) unchanged. No changes to signal engine, allocation targets, sleeve structure, or turnover rules. v2.9.3: Fat-tail gate fix for SIVR and IBIT. Per-ticker gate_sigma overrides added to per_ticker_thresholds — replaces EWMA 2.0/2.5σ global defaults for these two tickers only. SIVR: gate_sigma_buy=1.25, gate_sigma_sell=1.25. IBIT: gate_sigma_buy=1.5, gate_sigma_sell=1.5. Reviewed by ChatGPT + Gemini (1 round). Unanimous: reject 1.0σ sell for SIVR (too sensitive, ~16% fire rate), adopt 1.25σ. GPT: defer vol clamp (Fix 1b) and IAUM to separate review. Gemini: include both now. Reconciliation: Fix 1 only in v2.9.3; vol clamp and IAUM logged as future_review_items. No changes to signal engine, allocation targets, sleeve structure, turnover rules, or any other gate parameters. v2.9.2: Round 4 consensus — spike_trim_cap_interaction investigation closed. Path A (monitor-only) adopted by both reviewers. Automatic 90-day rolling event counter; escalation at >5% of spike_trim events activates contingent spike_trim_reentry_buffer (0.75% weight gap, momentum buys only, does not override compliance enforcement). No hard cooldown rule added. Execution gate declared production-ready by both reviewers (ChatGPT + Gemini, 4 rounds). No changes to signal engine, allocation targets, sleeve structure, or ticker constraints.

TREASURY_NOTE.notes: Treasury note must use live market pricing for Bucket A compliance checks. Hardcoded fallback price retained for system startup only. Compliance tolerance (acceptable deviation from par) TBD Round 2.

notes: Use residual cash to fund buys before generating any sells.
Sells-first refers to execution ordering only, after cash funding is applied.
Cash cannot satisfy sleeve floors or prevent hard cap enforcement; floors/caps still determine target weights.
If cash_available >= total_buys, then total_sells must be 0.

execution.notes: max_turnover (0.20) applies per rebalance event. max_turnover_stress (0.22) applies during soft_limit drawdown breach. max_turnover_annualized (0.60) is the binding annual ceiling — sufficient for two major portfolio rotations per year. turnover_reporting flags provide visibility when either cap is the operative constraint.

turnover_reporting.notes: Flags set to true at runtime when a rebalance is constrained by the annual or per-event turnover cap. Diagnostic telemetry only — does not block execution but requires acknowledgement in rebalance log.

trigger_precedence.description: When multiple triggers fire simultaneously, actions execute in this priority order. Higher-priority actions override or suspend lower-priority actions.

order.notes: Survival mode. Portfolio protection overrides all optimization.
Liquidity floor is non-negotiable. Restore before any rebalancing.

order.trigger: validators.l1_sleeve_caps OR validators.l2_sleeve_caps OR validators.l2_sleeve_floors OR validators.per_ticker_caps OR validators.per_ticker_floors
order.notes: Structural compliance. Caps and floors enforced before signal-driven trades.
If turnover cap binds, partial rebalance executed in priority order of violation severity.
Normal optimization. Executes only after all higher-priority constraints are satisfied.

lifecycle_rules.notes: Canonical intent: only inducted is allocatable. Activated is evaluation-only (non-allocatable), overlay is managed separately, reference is benchmark-only.

reference_to_inducted.notes: Ticker must pass observation period and show positive momentum before entering allocatable universe.

inducted_to_reference.notes: Demotion does not force immediate liquidation. Position winds down over exit_cycles.

re_entry_trigger.vix_trend_clause: TBD_round3: add VIX_5d_MA < VIX_20d_MA as alternative gate
re_entry_trigger.notes: VIX < 28 allows re-entry while market is elevated but not in panic. Intentionally more permissive than 25 to avoid missing face-ripping rallies in dormant names (particularly crypto) while VIX is falling from a peak. VIX trend clause (5d MA < 20d MA) to be added Round 3.

inducted_dormant.notes: Handles tickers that are inducted but not currently held (e.g. IBIT). Prevents discretionary re-entry without a signal.

any_to_activated.notes: Activated is a holding pen for tickers under review. Not allocatable.

ticker_constraints.notes: DEFAULT CONTRACT: Any ticker not explicitly present in ticker_constraints is reference-only (not held, not allocatable).

portfolio_accounting.notes: Contract: L1/L2 sleeve accounting uses only ticker_to_sleeves. Activated tickers intentionally omitted from ticker_to_sleeves do not affect sleeve cap/floor enforcement.

calendar.notes: Standing monthly rebalance regardless of band status.

band_breach.notes: Rebalance triggered when any tracked band is breached for hold_days_before_trigger consecutive days.

signal_drift.notes: Rebalance triggered when any sleeve drifts >5pp from target weight, or any ticker drifts >1.5pp from target weight. Both measured as absolute percentage point delta (actual minus target). Sleeve-level: e.g. ai_tech swelling from 32% to 37% triggers rebalance. Ticker-level: e.g. a 4%-capped ticker reaching 5.5% triggers trim.

stress_override.notes: Stress state suspends calendar and signal_drift triggers. Only band_breach enforcement continues.

allocation.denominator_definition: Allocatable denominator = TPV minus overlays (DBMF, KMLM) minus Bucket A (TREASURY_NOTE). Cash (Bucket B) IS included. Used as basis for all L1/L2 sleeve cap calculations.

cash_reserve.notes: Disabled: Bucket A is Treasuries-only; cash remains in Bucket B and may be idle.

growth.economic_driver: equity beta, AI capex, earnings growth
growth.notes: Core equity risk. Momentum model concentrates here in bull regimes.

real_assets.economic_driver: commodity cycle, electrification, geopolitics, energy demand
real_assets.notes: Pro-cyclical real asset and geopolitical equity exposure. Includes energy equities (XLE), defense contractors (ITA), and commodity miners (URNM, REMX, COPX). NOT a crisis hedge — these are growth/cycle trades.

monetary_hedges.economic_driver: real rates, inflation, currency debasement, crisis hedge
monetary_hedges.notes: True macro hedges. Gold and silver. Low equity correlation over long horizons. These are the only assets expected to hold/rise during equity drawdowns.

stabilizers.notes: Non-allocatable overlay sleeve. Excluded from L1/L2 caps and denominator. DBMF + KMLM managed futures. Bucket A (Treasuries) is governed separately as protected liquidity — it is not subject to this overlay band.

ai_tech.notes: AI hardware, infrastructure, and software ETFs.

core_equity.notes: Broad market index anchors. VTI has min_total floor via ticker_constraints.

strategic_materials.notes: Uranium, rare earths, copper. Pro-cyclical commodity miners. High beta — can drawdown 50%+ in commodity bear cycles. Capped at 10% L2 for concentration risk.

defense_energy.notes: Oil majors (XLE) and defense contractors (ITA). Geopolitical equity exposure. Cyclical with macro tailwinds. Reclassified from legacy 'defensive' L1 to real_assets L1 in v2.8.0.

precious_metals.notes: Gold (IAUM) and Silver (SIVR). True macro hedges. Isolated from commodity cycle assets in v2.8.0 restructure. Gold competes only with silver within this sleeve.

crypto.notes: Hard cap. IBIT only. Currently dormant (score negative).

managed_futures.notes: Overlay sleeve. Target split handled by overlays.managed_futures.

XLE.notes: Reclassified from geopolitical_stress overlay to defense_energy sleeve 2026-03-04. Max_total 8% consistent with ITA. Excluded_from_denominator removed — now included in Bucket B.

QQQ.notes: Benchmark Proxy only (not held / not allocatable)

IBIT.notes: Inducted. Fully allocatable via momentum engine.

momentum_to_weight.notes: Momentum scores percentile-ranked over 63-day lookback within inducted universe. Rank maps linearly to target weight within [min_total, max_total] band. Linear scaling is the conservative baseline — revisit convex scaling in Round 3 once actual allocation output can be evaluated. Floor exit: reduce position to zero after 20 consecutive days of negative blend. Exit threshold (20d) intentionally longer than re-entry threshold (15d) — requires more conviction to exit than to re-enter.

condition_notes.brent_1d_pct_change_gte: 6% single-day Brent spike = acute supply shock (missile strike, port closure, sudden embargo). Catches the panic bid.
condition_notes.brent_3d_cumulative_pct_change_gte: 8% 3-day cumulative Brent move = sustained escalation (slow-rolling naval blockade, multi-day Hormuz disruption). Catches what the single-day threshold misses.
condition_notes.vix_inversion: Term structure inversion signals acute near-term panic premium.

geopolitical_trigger_definition.notes: ADVISORY ONLY. Trigger flags a review window; execution remains manual and discretionary within band_total [0.02, 0.06]. XLE position managed via GTC limit orders, not algorithmic execution.

geopolitical_stress.notes: Disabled 2026-03-04. XLE reclassified back to defense_energy sleeve. Geopolitical overlay structure retained for potential future use.

default_reference_contract.notes: Any ticker absent from ticker_constraints must be treated as reference-only via governance.defaults.ticker_constraints.

l1_sleeve_caps.notes: L1 sleeve totals (% of allocatable denom) must not exceed level1[sleeve].cap. Basis: allocatable_denominator = TPV - overlays - Bucket A.

l2_sleeve_caps.notes: L2 sleeve weights (% of allocatable denom) must not exceed level2[sleeve].cap. Basis: allocatable_denominator.

l2_sleeve_floors.notes: L2 sleeve weights (% of allocatable denom) must not fall below level2[sleeve].floor. Basis: allocatable_denominator (TPV minus overlays minus Bucket A; cash included). Floor conflict resolution: if the optimization target set is infeasible — defined as: cannot simultaneously satisfy all hard constraints (Bucket A minimum, per-ticker caps, per-ticker min_total floors, overlay bands, turnover cap) given current TPV and allocatable denominator — then L2 sleeve floors are treated as soft constraints and relaxed proportionally. Hard constraints are never relaxed except as explicitly overridden by drawdown_rules. Tie-break precedence: satisfy per-ticker min_total (TPV-based) first; remaining capital is then allocated to meet sleeve floors proportionally. No engine may reduce a ticker below its min_total in order to satisfy a sleeve floor unless drawdown hard_limit is active (see drawdown_rules.hard_limit_action).

per_ticker_caps.notes: Individual ticker weights (as % of TOTAL PORTFOLIO VALUE / TPV) must not exceed ticker_constraints[ticker].max_total. Basis: TPV (includes overlays and Bucket A).

per_ticker_floors.notes: Individual ticker weights (as % of TPV) must not fall below ticker_constraints[ticker].min_total. Basis: TPV.

per_ticker_bands.notes: Tickers with band_total must remain within [band_total[0], band_total[1]]. Currently: none.

funding_invariant.notes: Must hold for every rebalance event.

turnover_limit.notes: Warning on per-event breach (>20%). Error if annualized cap (60%) is projected to be breached within current calendar year. Sets turnover_reporting flags.

bucket_a_minimum.notes: Must use market price, not fixed fallback price, for this check.

drawdown_limit.notes: Soft breach triggers buy freeze. Hard breach triggers forced reduction to floors.

signal_staleness.notes: Warn if momentum signal inputs (price data) have not been updated within max_stale_days.

momentum_data_missing.notes: Warn if any inducted ticker has insufficient price history to compute momentum blend.

lifecycle_review_overdue.notes: Warn if any inducted ticker has not been reviewed within lifecycle_rules.review_days_default days.

rebalance_trigger_overdue.notes: Warn if calendar rebalance has not been acted upon within 3 business days of scheduled date (first Monday of month).

bucket_a_protected_liquidity.name: Bucket A — Protected Liquidity
bucket_a_protected_liquidity.excluded_assets: Cash does not satisfy Bucket A

bucket_b_deployable.name: Bucket B — Deployable Capital
bucket_b_deployable.notes: Basis for allocatable denominator (cash included; overlays excluded; Bucket A excluded). All non-Treasury assets plus cash. Deployable capital for momentum allocation.

allocatable_ex_bucket_a.notes: Allocatable denominator = TPV minus overlays (DBMF, KMLM) minus Bucket A (TREASURY_NOTE). Cash (Bucket B) is included. Overlays excluded because they are non-budgeted. Used as basis for all L1/L2 sleeve cap and floor calculations.

objectives.notes: Primary: outperform VTI by ≥150bps net of estimated turnover costs. Cost model assumes 8bps per dollar traded (spread + slippage for liquid ETFs). Secondary: limit peak-to-trough drawdown per drawdown_rules.

_meta.notes: Execution timing filter. Does not change target weights or momentum signals. Defers or accelerates trade execution based on statistical extremity of the 2-day price move relative to each ticker's own EWMA volatility. Spike-trim is informational for cap-breach sells — execution remains unconditional; annotation flags optimal timing only.

short_term_confirmation.vol_clamp_note: v2.9.4: effective_vol = clamp(ewma_vol, 0.75×RV1y, 1.50×RV1y). Validated against 2019–2026 history (COVID 2020, rate shock 2022). Prevents gate over-sensitivity in compressed-vol regimes (floor) and gate lock-up during velocity shocks (ceiling). See future_review_items.ewma_regime_shift_vol_clamp for full F1 validation.
short_term_confirmation.vol_conversion_derivation: daily_vol = annual_vol / sqrt(252); 2d_vol = daily_vol * sqrt(2); simplified: annual_vol / sqrt(126). Both forms are equivalent.

stress_regime_overrides.rationale: During soft_limit drawdown, prolonged sell-deferral conflicts with the goal of risk reduction. Sell-side capitulation protection is shortened from 10 to 3 calendar days so the portfolio can reduce exposure within a tight window even in stress. Buy-defer window unchanged — freezing new buys is already enforced by stress_override; the 10-day buy-defer does not add material constraint. Both reviewers reviewed this interaction: ChatGPT recommended disabling sell-defer entirely; Gemini recommended accepting the full freeze. Resolved as a middle path — sell-defer preserved at compressed 3-day window to protect against worst panic-day execution while ensuring risk reduction within 3 days.

turnover_accounting.description: A deferred trade's dollar volume counts against the turnover cap of the rebalance event that originally generated it, not the event during which it eventually executes. If the deferred trade executes between scheduled rebalances (e.g., at max_defer_calendar_days expiry), its turnover is logged as a post-event adjustment to the originating cycle's turnover ledger. It does not consume turnover budget for any subsequent rebalance event.
turnover_accounting.rationale: Prevents a deferred trade from causing a cascade: if it counted against the next rebalance, that rebalance could be artificially constrained by the turnover cap, clipping otherwise valid momentum trades. Both reviewers independently flagged this gap. Gemini provided the attribution-to-originating-cycle framing; ChatGPT confirmed it must not count against the next event.
turnover_accounting.implementation_note: Engine must tag each trade with originating_rebalance_id at signal generation time, not at execution time. Execution engine posts turnover to originating_rebalance_id regardless of when settlement occurs.

asymmetric_sigma.rationale: False sell-deferral (holding deteriorating position) is more costly than false buy-deferral (missing a buy entry). Asymmetry has principled basis. Both reviewers considered; Gemini recommended, ChatGPT suggested defer. Adopted upfront — zero implementation cost.

max_defer_units.rationale: Both reviewers independently recommended calendar days over rebalance cycles. Frequency-independent. Stress regime shortens sell-defer to 3 days to allow timely risk reduction.

fat_tail_tickers.rationale: v2.9.3: Per-ticker gate_sigma overrides adopted for SIVR and IBIT after 1-round LLM review (ChatGPT + Gemini). SIVR sell-side gap was 10.82pp (EWMA 2.5σ = −17.71% vs empirical p2.5 = −6.89%) — capitulation protection functionally disabled. IBIT buy-side gap 2.36pp. Unanimous: Fix 1 (sigma override), reject Fix 3 (Student-t), SIVR gate_sigma_sell = 1.25 not 1.0 (1.0σ fires ~16% of days). Split on Fix 1b (vol clamp) and IAUM — deferred. Recalibration: annual base + manual exception if ewma_emp_gap > 3pp buy-side or 5pp sell-side for 2 consecutive monthly runs. Prior decision (v2.9.2): keep_ewma_uniform — both reviewers agreed EWMA uniform acceptable given position caps. Fat-tail issue deferred with monitor flag.

recalibration.exception_trigger: ewma_emp_gap > 3pp buy-side OR > 5pp sell-side for 2 consecutive monthly runs
recalibration.exception_action: manual gate_sigma recalibration and policy version bump

spike_trim_scope.rationale: Gemini recommended extension; ChatGPT preferred momentum-only. Resolved as informational annotation only — cap-breach sells execute unconditionally regardless.

stress_sell_defer.rationale: ChatGPT: risk reduction must not be blocked. Gemini: panic execution costs are highest — freeze is beneficial. Middle path: 3-day sell-defer window preserves protection against worst-day execution while ensuring risk reduction within 3 calendar days, not 10.

turnover_accounting.rationale: Both reviewers confirmed gap. Gemini provided originating-cycle framing; ChatGPT confirmed must not count against next event.

vol_conversion_documentation.rationale: Both reviewers recommended explicit derivation for auditability. Gemini proposed compact form; both forms now documented.

mechanisms: Mean-reversion tilt: systematic sell-deferral during drawdowns causes portfolio to hold losers longer than momentum signal intends.
Trade supersession: deferred sell may never execute if fresh rebalance recomputes a smaller gap at a recovered price.

Signal drift amplification (ChatGPT Round 2, new): actual_weight > intended_weight post-deferral means future rank changes have larger-than-intended portfolio impact, introducing path dependency in the allocation engine.
hidden_gate_allocation_interaction.magnitude: Small. Sell-defer fires at 2.5sigma (~0.6% of days per ticker). 10-day expiry limits persistence. Estimated 2-4% of sells deferred annually.
hidden_gate_allocation_interaction.net_effect: Mild momentum × mean-reversion overlay. Not a bug. Accepted as structural trade-off of execution timing protection.
hidden_gate_allocation_interaction.action: Monitor via rebalance logs. Quantify in Round 3 simulation over multi-cycle backtest.

spike_trim_cap_interaction.mechanism: Cap-boundary oscillation: spike-trim execution at P_spike > P_signal may cause post-trim weight to undershoot target, triggering a compensating buy at next rebalance on the same ticker. Conditions required: ticker near cap + short-term spike >= 2sigma + momentum rank persistent. Most likely tickers: SOXQ, CHAT, SIVR, URNM.
spike_trim_cap_interaction.frequency_estimate: < 1% of trades. Bounded structurally by existing 20% turnover cap (round-trip consumes two slots in the same cycle).
spike_trim_cap_interaction.directionality: Not necessarily harmful. SELL at spike then BUY slightly lower is often a profitable micro-rebalance capturing the volatility premium. Harmful only if spike persists without mean-reversion — less common than reversion in short windows.
spike_trim_cap_interaction.path_decision: Path A — monitor with automatic counter and escalation. No cooldown rule added. Both reviewers endorsed after ChatGPT directionality and turnover-cap-adequacy arguments.

review_decisions.chatgpt_round4: Path A endorsed. Monitoring sufficient. Cooldown would introduce signal-suppression artifact (strong winners held below target), arguably worse than the rare oscillation.
review_decisions.gemini_round4: Path A endorsed — conceded directionality and turnover-cap arguments. Hard cooldown ruled out.

avg_days_between_spike_trim_and_reentry.rationale: Distinguishes 1-cycle oscillations (short-lived spike reversals) from multi-cycle patterns (persistent trend breaks). Helps diagnose whether oscillation events are benign mean-reversion captures or structural execution drag. Source: ChatGPT Round 4 operational suggestion.

contingent_mitigation.description: Buy allowed only if target_weight - actual_weight >= 0.75%. Prevents momentum-driven rebuy of a recently spike-trimmed ticker that is only marginally below target.

notes: Asymmetric sigma: sell_defer at 2.5sigma; buy_defer at 2.0sigma.
Stress regime: sell_defer window shortened to 3 calendar days when soft_limit active. Buy_defer unchanged at 10 days.
Turnover: deferred trade counted against originating rebalance event, not execution event. Engine must tag trades with originating_rebalance_id.

SIVR and IBIT: fat_tail_fixed via per_ticker_gate_sigma_override (v2.9.3). SIVR gate_sigma_buy=1.25, gate_sigma_sell=1.25 (gates: buy 8.86%, sell 8.86%). IBIT gate_sigma_buy=1.5, gate_sigma_sell=1.5 (gates: buy 6.93%, sell 6.93%). IAUM: fat_tail_monitor only (gap 1.58pp, below 2pp threshold — deferred).
Spike-trim fires when z >= +2.0 on sell-direction trade. Execution immediate. Applies to both momentum and cap-breach sells.
Gate fires on ~2.5% of trading days per ticker (buy-side). Sell-side 2.5sigma fires on ~0.6% of days.
Vol derivation: daily_vol = annual_vol / sqrt(252); 2d_vol = daily_vol * sqrt(2) = annual_vol / sqrt(126).

per_ticker_thresholds._method: ewma_126d_annualised / sqrt(126) applied asymmetrically: buy_defer at 2.0sigma, sell_defer at 2.5sigma
per_ticker_thresholds._note: Thresholds recomputed at each rebalance from live EWMA vol. Values below are reference snapshot only.

monitoring_design._note: Three-layer monitoring framework. Recommended by ChatGPT post-v2.9.4 review (2026-03-11), referencing Hendricks VaR evaluation, ESRB procyclicality, and WFE impulse-response literature.

tail_calibration_drift.description: Per-ticker tail calibration audit. Tracks empirical p2.5/p97.5 for 2-day returns, implied effective sigma (quantile ÷ vol estimate), and pp gap vs current gate thresholds.

new_v294_addition.definition: Number of 2-day return observations in the calibration window that fall above emp_p97.5 (buy-side) or below emp_p2.5 (sell-side).
new_v294_addition.rationale: Hendricks (1996): extreme quantile estimates can be driven by only a few observations and shift abruptly with finite windows. If n_tail < 5, flag the pp gap as statistically unreliable regardless of magnitude.
new_v294_addition.alert_threshold: n_tail < 5 → suppress recalibration recommendation; add 'low_sample' flag to audit output

tail_calibration_drift.action: No change to gate parameters until pp gap exceeds threshold for 2 consecutive monthly runs AND n_tail >= 5.

clamp_binding_diagnostics.frequency: monthly, plus event-driven after any vol spike
clamp_binding_diagnostics.description: Per-ticker clamp activation statistics computed from gate evaluation logs.
metrics: floor_binding_rate_pct: % of gate evaluations in period where vol_clamp_type == 'floor'
ceiling_binding_rate_pct: % of gate evaluations in period where vol_clamp_type == 'ceiling'
clamp_impact_ratio: mean(effective_vol_2d / raw_vol_2d) for the period — 1.0 = no clamp effect
clamp_binding_diagnostics.rationale: Measures 'tool activation' analogous to procyclicality-mitigation frameworks (Bank of Canada, ESRB). Detects if clamp is binding more frequently than expected, which could signal a regime shift warranting recalibration of 0.75×/1.50× bounds.
clamp_binding_diagnostics.alert_threshold: ceiling_binding_rate_pct > 15% for any ticker over a rolling 63-day window → flag for review. Floor > 30% for any ticker → flag for review.
clamp_binding_diagnostics.data_source: vol_clamp_type field in gate evaluation return dicts (logged per trade)

outcome_based_kpis.description: Gate effectiveness metrics computed from mws_run_results.csv. Measures whether the gate is adding or subtracting value relative to immediate execution.
metrics: deferral_rate_by_ticker_direction: % of gate evaluations resulting in 'defer' per ticker/BUY/SELL
mean_defer_duration_days: average calendar days between defer decision and eventual execution
slippage_proxy: (execution_price - signal_time_price) / signal_time_price, sign-adjusted for direction — positive = gate improved entry/exit timing
outcome_based_kpis.rationale: Industry volatility-control methodologies add turnover buffers because volatility-estimation noise causes churn. Quarterly KPI review verifies the vol clamp (v2.9.4) does not increase deferral frequency or churn in calm periods (floor binding side-effect check).
outcome_based_kpis.implementation_status: pending — requires lightweight audit script reading mws_run_results.csv. No runner changes needed.

drawdown_rules.soft_limit_action: freeze_new_buys_review_required. Per-ticker min_total constraints remain in force during soft limit. No forced selling triggered at soft limit.
drawdown_rules.hard_limit_action: reduce_all_positions toward sleeve floors, subject to per-ticker min_total constraints. Exception: if reducing to sleeve floors is insufficient to exit hard_limit breach, per-ticker min_total may be overridden. In all cases Bucket A minimum is preserved. Hard limit override of min_total must be logged as a compliance exception.
drawdown_rules.notes: Soft limit (20%): freeze new buys, require manual review. Hard limit (28%): force reduction of all positions to sleeve floors. Recovery: resume normal rebalancing once drawdown < 12% for 10 consecutive days. 20% soft aligns with conventional bear market definition. 8pp gap (20→28) provides managed response window before hard limit fires.

constraint_precedence_during_drawdown.priority_1_always: Bucket A minimum (protected liquidity — never touched)
constraint_precedence_during_drawdown.priority_4_normal: L2 sleeve floors (soft — relaxed proportionally if infeasible)
constraint_precedence_during_drawdown.hard_limit_override: hard_limit_action may override per_ticker_min_total if reducing to sleeve floors is insufficient to exit breach. Override must be logged.

future_review_items._note: Items logged for future policy review cycles. Not active constraints. Not part of execution gate.

ewma_regime_shift_vol_clamp.source: ChatGPT fat-tail review (2026-03-11), confirmed by Gemini. Full F1 validation completed 2026-03-11 against extended history (2019–2026).
ewma_regime_shift_vol_clamp.description: EWMA volatility lags true volatility during regime transitions, causing z-score gate to oscillate between blindness (vol spike: EWMA slow to rise, threshold widens, gate stops firing) and over-firing (post-crisis: EWMA slow to fall, routine moves score as statistically extreme). Proposed fix (Fix 1b): clamp effective_vol = clamp(ewma_vol, 0.75 × realized_vol_1y, 1.50 × realized_vol_1y). Affects all tickers but disproportionate impact on SIVR and IBIT due to faster vol regime cycling.
ewma_regime_shift_vol_clamp.action: IMPLEMENT in next policy version bump (v2.9.4). Bounds validated. See proposed_implementation.
ewma_regime_shift_vol_clamp.proposed_implementation: effective_vol_2d = clamp(ewma_vol_2d, 0.75 × realized_vol_2d_1y, 1.50 × realized_vol_2d_1y); z = ret_2d / effective_vol_2d. ~3 runner lines. Add vol_clamp_floor_multiplier and vol_clamp_ceiling_multiplier to execution_gates.short_term_confirmation block.

full_validation_results.data_window: 2019-01-02 to 2026-03-10 (1806 trading days for core tickers)
full_validation_results.method: Per-ticker: EWMA vol (span=126, annualised), RV1y (252-day rolling std × sqrt(252)). Vol clamp: floor=0.75×RV1y, ceiling=1.50×RV1y. Stress windows: COVID_onset_2020 (2020-01-02 to 2020-04-30), RateShock_onset_2022 (2021-10-01 to 2022-10-15).

boc_concern_test.question: Does EWMA spike above the ceiling (1.50×RV1y_calm) within the first 30 trading days of a stress onset?
boc_concern_test.verdict: NOT CONFIRMED EMPIRICALLY. After extending mws_ticker_history.csv back to 2019, the Bank of Canada concern became testable for the first time (it required stress onsets with a calm prior-year RV1y window — absent in the Sep 2024–Mar 2026 data). EWMA/RV1y ratios in the first 30 trading days of both stress onsets were 0.86–1.11 — well below the 1.50× ceiling. The scenario (EWMA spiking above a calm-window ceiling at onset) did not materialise in either 2020 COVID or 2022 rate shock. 1y RV1y lookback is sufficient; no need to extend to 3y or 5y unconditional vol. Resolved as: concern not empirically confirmed on 7-year dataset spanning two stress regimes.

VTI.note: Ceiling binds mid-COVID as EWMA spikes well above 1.50×RV1y. Clamp suppresses effective_vol from 52.6% to 46.9% at peak — prevents gate from locking up entirely during maximum drawdown.
Slow grind — EWMA rises in tandem with RV1y, never exceeds ceiling. Clamp has zero effect on slow-moving bear markets.

pass_criteria_evaluation.criterion_1_ceiling_lt_5pct_per_ticker: PASS — all tickers 0–2.9% overall ceiling binding
pass_criteria_evaluation.criterion_2_floor_binds_in_compressed_vo: PASS — VTI 11.7%, COPX 4.6%, SOXX 4.0% floor binding confirms gate protection during quiet regimes following elevated vol
pass_criteria_evaluation.criterion_3_ceiling_binds_during_vel_spi: PASS — ceiling binding confirmed during COVID peak for 5 of 6 tickers tested; correctly absent during 2022 slow grind

full_validation_results.key_insight: The ceiling binding during COVID occurs mid-stress (April 2020), NOT at onset. By this point the clamp is functioning correctly: it caps EWMA to prevent extreme z-scores that would lock up the gate entirely during maximum drawdown. The 10–11% vol suppression on VTI and COPX at peak stress keeps z-scores within a range where sell-defer continues to function rather than blocking all trades. The 2022 rate shock shows no clamp interaction at all, confirming the clamp does not distort the gate during normal slow-moving bear markets.
full_validation_results.verdict: PASS. Both 0.75× floor and 1.50× ceiling bounds are well-calibrated across 7 years of history including two major stress regimes. Bank of Canada calm-window concern is not empirically confirmed. Implement Fix 1b in v2.9.4.

iaum_fat_tail_monitoring.description: IAUM (gold) has ewma_emp_gap of 1.58pp (emp_p975 = 3.86%, EWMA 2σ = 5.44%). Below the 2pp intervention threshold used to select SIVR/IBIT for v2.9.3 fix. Gemini argued for inclusion (same precious-metals fat-tail structure as SIVR); GPT argued for exclusion (below threshold, keeps patch crisp). Reconciliation: exclude from v2.9.3, log for monitoring. If IAUM gap breaches 2pp buy-side or 3pp sell-side, recalibrate alongside next SIVR/IBIT recalibration cycle.
iaum_fat_tail_monitoring.action: Monitor ewma_emp_gap for IAUM in rebalance run logs. Trigger review if gap exceeds 2pp buy-side or 3pp sell-side. Sell-gap is currently 2.97pp (borderline). Run calibration audit after each monthly rebalance until sell-gap stabilises below 2.5pp.

current_gaps.note: Sell-gap at 2.97pp — 0.03pp from the 3pp monitoring trigger. Buy-gap at 1.24pp — 0.76pp from 2pp trigger. Measured against 252-day empirical window. Recommend measuring again after next monthly run.

allocation_layer_sleeve_constraint_inter.description: percentile_in_band + sleeve-constraint interaction may create systematic over-weighting of mid-rank assets during regime transitions. When a regime shift moves momentum ranks sharply, mid-rank tickers within a sleeve that is at its L1 cap receive proportionally more weight than their updated rank warrants, because the reallocation is bounded by the cap. Effect: short-lived over-weight in transitional assets during the rebalance cycle immediately following a regime shift.
allocation_layer_sleeve_constraint_inter.action: Investigate in next allocation-engine policy review. Quantify via regime-transition backtests.

urnm_buy_gate_monitoring.description: URNM (uranium miners) buy-side EWMA/empirical gap at +1.74pp (emp_p97.5 = 11.19% vs EWMA 2σ gate = 9.45%). Below the 3pp recalibration threshold but trending in direction of fat-tail divergence. Sell-side gap is -2.73pp (gate wider than empirical). Back-solved buy sigma = 2.37 (vs global 2.0). If buy-gap exceeds 3pp: add URNM to per_ticker_thresholds with gate_sigma_buy override.
urnm_buy_gate_monitoring.action: Include URNM in monthly calibration audit output. No action until buy_gap_pp > 3pp for 2 consecutive monthly runs.

news_intelligence._note: Framework for incorporating real-world news and market context into LLM-based run execution. The Python runner (mws_titanium_runner.py) computes momentum scores, charts, and breach alerts. The LLM is the actual execution engine that produces final recommendations. News is an advisory overlay — it modifies execution decisions but does not override hard policy rules.

architecture.news_role: modifier — confirms or challenges systematic signal; informs discretionary override candidates; provides rationale context
architecture.hard_rule: News alone CANNOT override hard_limit drawdown rules, cap/floor constraints, Bucket A minimums, or turnover caps. News CAN trigger a discretionary_override_review for any trade.
architecture.data_file: mws_market_context.md — AUTO-GENERATED by LLM runner at run start via web search. Never populated by user. LLM searches, rates, and writes this file before producing recommendations.

macro_rates_inflation.description: Federal Reserve communications, CPI/PCE/jobs data, yield curve moves, global central bank policy
high_materiality_triggers: Unexpected Fed rate decision or forward guidance shift
CPI/PCE surprise > 0.3pp vs consensus
Yield curve inversion or dis-inversion
Recession signal from 2+ leading indicators simultaneously

geopolitical.description: Armed conflict escalation, sanctions, trade restrictions, energy supply disruptions
high_materiality_triggers: New sanctions on commodity-producing nations (uranium, rare earth, copper)
Major military escalation involving energy-producing regions
Trade restriction on semiconductors or critical minerals
Energy supply disruption affecting >2% of global supply

ai_tech_policy.description: AI regulation, semiconductor export controls, major model releases, compute demand signals, antitrust actions
high_materiality_triggers: New chip export restriction (US, Netherlands, Japan)
Major antitrust action against AI/semiconductor company
Paradigm-shifting model release that resets compute demand curve
National AI strategy shift in US, EU, or China

energy_transition.description: Grid investment policy, nuclear licensing, EV adoption, energy storage breakthroughs, carbon pricing
high_materiality_triggers: Nuclear plant approval or cancellation affecting uranium demand
Major grid investment bill passage or repeal
Significant EV demand data miss or beat affecting battery metals

crypto_regulatory.description: SEC/CFTC enforcement actions, ETF structure changes, exchange failures, stablecoin regulation
high_materiality_triggers: SEC/CFTC action directly targeting Bitcoin ETF structure or custody
Major exchange insolvency or fraud
Stablecoin regulatory framework passage affecting crypto market structure

biotech_fda.description: FDA calendar, major clinical trial readouts, drug approval/rejection, CMS coverage decisions
high_materiality_triggers: FDA rejection of major drug class affecting XBI basket significantly
Surprise approval or accelerated review for major platform technology
CMS coverage decision materially affecting sector revenue

precious_metals_macro.description: Real interest rates, DXY moves, central bank gold buying, inflation expectations, safe-haven flows
high_materiality_triggers: Real rates move > 30bp in a week
DXY move > 2% in a week
Major central bank gold buying/selling announcement
Significant shift in 5y5y inflation breakevens

managed_futures_regime.description: Trend environment quality, cross-asset momentum, CTA positioning, correlation regime shifts
high_materiality_triggers: Sharp cross-asset correlation spike (equity-bond correlation turns positive unexpectedly)
Trend reversal signal across 3+ asset classes simultaneously
Major CTA fund liquidation event

HIGH.definition: News that a reasonable investor would expect to materially affect sleeve-level returns over the next 30–90 days. May change allocation recommendation or trigger override review.
HIGH.llm_action: Assess whether news CONFIRMS or CONTRADICTS current momentum signal for affected sleeve. Document explicitly in recommendation. If contradicts: state override candidate and rationale. If confirms: note as conviction booster.

MEDIUM.definition: Relevant context that adds nuance but does not alter the systematic recommendation. Informs risk awareness.
MEDIUM.llm_action: Document in news_overlay_summary. No change to recommendation unless combined with other MEDIUM items pointing the same direction.

LOW.definition: Background awareness. Does not affect current run.
LOW.llm_action: Log in market context file. Not required in recommendation output.

confirms_momentum.definition: News directionally consistent with the current momentum signal for the affected sleeve
confirms_momentum.example: Strong AI capex announcements when ai_tech sleeve has high momentum rank
confirms_momentum.llm_action: Note as conviction booster in rationale. No change to target weights — signal already captures this.

contradicts_momentum.definition: News directionally inconsistent with current momentum signal
contradicts_momentum.example: Major semiconductor export ban announced when ai_tech sleeve has high momentum and would be a BUY
contradicts_momentum.llm_action: Flag as override_candidate. State: (1) what the systematic signal says, (2) what the news implies, (3) recommendation on whether to follow signal or invoke discretionary override, (4) explicit rationale. Default to following signal unless news is HIGH materiality AND not yet priced (< 2 trading days old).

novel_not_yet_priced.definition: Breaking news within 2 trading days that has not yet been reflected in momentum signals
novel_not_yet_priced.llm_action: Highest scrutiny. Explicitly state 'signal may not yet reflect this news.' Consider deferring the affected trade by 2–5 trading days to allow price discovery, OR invoke discretionary override with documented rationale.
novel_not_yet_priced.governance: Any trade deferred or overridden due to novel news must be logged in mws_tracker.json with news_override flag.

required_recommendation_output.description: Every LLM run recommendation must include a news_overlay_summary section

format.items_reviewed: List of HIGH and MEDIUM news items considered, with category and materiality rating
format.signal_interactions: For each HIGH item: CONFIRMS or CONTRADICTS momentum signal, and for which sleeve
format.override_candidates: Any trades flagged for discretionary override review due to news, with rationale
format.net_news_stance: One-line summary: e.g., 'News context is broadly supportive of current momentum signals. No override candidates.' or 'Two HIGH items contradict ai_tech BUY signal — see override candidates.'

required_recommendation_output.default_stance: If mws_market_context.md is not populated or is stale (> 5 business days old), note this in recommendation and proceed on systematic signals only.

news_weight_governance.maximum_news_influence: News can defer a trade up to 5 business days pending price discovery, or trigger a discretionary override review. It cannot unilaterally cancel a trade that passes all policy constraints.
news_cannot_do: Override hard_limit or soft_limit drawdown controls
Increase total position size beyond turnover cap
news_can_do: Flag a momentum trade as override_candidate with documented rationale
Defer a specific trade execution by up to 5 business days (novel news only)
Inform sleeve-level conviction in written recommendation
Trigger a policy review request for a structural change

generation_protocol._note: Defines exactly how the LLM runner generates mws_market_context.md at the start of every run.
generation_protocol.trigger: FIRST step of every MWS run, before computing any recommendation.
generation_protocol.output_disposition: overwrite — this file is always regenerated fresh each run
generation_protocol.time_window: trailing 7 calendar days unless a HIGH-materiality item requires longer context (max 30 days)

macro_rates_inflation: Federal Reserve interest rate decision OR minutes site:federalreserve.gov OR site:wsj.com OR site:ft.com -7d
CPI PCE inflation data release surprise -7d
recession indicator GDP PMI leading indicator -7d
geopolitical: sanctions commodity uranium rare earth copper -7d
ai_tech_policy: SOXQ BOTZ CHAT DTCR GRID earnings OR news -7d
energy_transition: nuclear plant license approval uranium demand -7d
EV demand battery metals lithium copper -7d
biotech_fda: XBI biotech ETF clinical trial readout -7d
precious_metals_macro: gold silver real interest rates TIPS -7d
central bank gold buying selling reserves -7d

rating_rules.HIGH: Triggers matching any item in the category's high_materiality_triggers list, OR LLM judges >20bp expected sleeve impact over 30-90 days.
rating_rules.MEDIUM: Relevant, directionally clear, but expected <20bp sleeve impact or already partially priced.
rating_rules.LOW: Background context. Log but do not include in recommendation output.
rating_rules.default_if_uncertain: MEDIUM — do not over-rate; only escalate to HIGH when the high_materiality_trigger criteria are clearly met.

output_format.header: # MWS Market Context — AUTO-GENERATED {date}\n_Generated by LLM runner. Do not edit manually. Overwritten each run._
per_category: | ... rows, one per material news item |
**LLM notes:** {free text, required for HIGH items, optional for MEDIUM}
footer_sections: ## Override Candidates — list HIGH-materiality contradictions with affected ticker, direction, rationale
## Market Regime Snapshot — VIX level + 5d trend, TPV drawdown from mws_tracker.json, risk regime

generation_protocol.search_failure_handling: If web search is unavailable or returns no results for a category, write 'NO DATA — web search unavailable' in that category block. Do not fabricate news. Proceed with systematic signal only for that sleeve.
